title: Add reduced-motion fallback
summary: The codebase already detects `prefers-reduced-motion` and maps it to `motionAmplitude: 0.2`. However, several motion sources in the particle system are NOT scaled by this multiplier — treble-driven jitter, sparkle shimmer, and pointer disturbance all bypass the reduced-motion gate. This plan closes those gaps and adds a live listener so the preference is honoured if toggled mid-session.

alreadyImplemented: Velocity scaling: `speed = cadence * motionAmplitude * delta * 0.06` in `src/visual/systems/particleField.ts:55`
CSS transitions disabled for UI elements in `src/style.css:99-105`
gaps: Pointer disturbance (`particleField.ts:66-67`) is applied without `motionAmplitude` scaling
Treble-driven micro-jitter (`particleField.ts:94-97`) is applied without `motionAmplitude` scaling
Sparkle shimmer pulsing (`particleField.ts:84-86`) is applied without `motionAmplitude` scaling
No live listener — if user toggles reduced-motion mid-session, the change is not picked up
No dedicated US-024 acceptance test file

steps.title: Add live `prefers-reduced-motion` listener to signals module
steps.details: Export a new function `watchReducedMotion(cb: (prefersReduced: boolean) => void): () => void` that attaches a `change` listener on `matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')` and calls the callback when the preference changes. Return an unsubscribe function. This keeps detection centralised in the signals module.
steps.reason: Acceptance criterion: 'Reduced-motion preference is detected' — detection should be reactive, not just at init time.

steps.title: Wire live listener into the render loop
steps.details: In the `startLoop` function (or wherever `LoopDeps` is consumed), call `watchReducedMotion` to update `deps.signals.prefersReducedMotion` when the OS preference changes mid-session. Since `mapSignalsToVisuals` is called every frame with the current signals, the new value will flow through automatically. Store the unsubscribe function and call it in any existing cleanup/stop logic.
steps.reason: Ensures the mapping reacts in real time without requiring a page reload.

steps.title: Scale pointer disturbance by `motionAmplitude`
steps.reason: Pointer disturbance causes visible particle displacement — should be dampened for reduced-motion users.

steps.title: Dampen treble-driven micro-jitter with `motionAmplitude`
steps.reason: Rapid per-frame jitter is the most visually aggressive motion — must be reduced for comfort.

steps.title: Dampen sparkle shimmer pulsing with `motionAmplitude`
steps.details:
On line 86, scale the sparkle contribution by `motionAmplitude`:
```ts
const size = p.size * shimmer * (1 + sparklePhase * params.trebleEnergy * 0.5 * params.motionAmplitude);
```
This preserves the base size (visual identity) while reducing the oscillating size pulsing.
steps.reason: Size pulsing is perceptible motion. Dampening it preserves particle presence while reducing flicker.

steps.title: Write acceptance tests for US-024
steps.details:
Create a dedicated test file covering all four acceptance criteria:

1. **T-024-01: Reduced-motion preference is detected** — verify `readSignals()` returns `prefersReducedMotion: true` when the media query matches (may already pass via existing test, but include for completeness).
2. **T-024-02: Motion amplitude is lowered** — call `mapSignalsToVisuals` with `prefersReducedMotion: true`, assert `motionAmplitude <= 0.3` and `> 0`.
3. **T-024-03: Particle displacement is lower with reduced motion** — create two particle fields, run `draw()` with identical frames but `motionAmplitude: 1.0` vs `0.2`, assert total displacement is lower.
4. **T-024-04: Jitter and shimmer are dampened** — with `motionAmplitude: 0.2` and non-zero `trebleEnergy`, verify jitter offset calculation produces values < 20% of full-motion equivalent.
5. **T-024-05: Pointer disturbance is dampened** — with `motionAmplitude: 0.2` and non-zero `pointerDisturbance`, verify displacement is proportionally reduced.
6. **T-024-06: Core visual identity is preserved** — with reduced motion, verify particles still render (count > 0), hue/saturation/density are unchanged, and `motionAmplitude > 0`.
7. **T-024-07: No separate page required** — verify the same `mapSignalsToVisuals` function handles both cases (no alternate code path or page).
steps.reason: Covers all four acceptance criteria with verifiable assertions.

steps.title: Test the live listener
steps.details:
Add test cases for `watchReducedMotion`:
- T-024-08: Callback fires when the media query match changes.
- T-024-09: Unsubscribe function removes the listener (no callback after unsubscribe).
steps.reason: Ensures the reactive listener works correctly and cleans up.

steps.title: Verify build and run full test suite
steps.details: Run `npm run build` and `npx vitest run` to confirm no build errors, no console errors, and all tests pass (including existing reduced-motion tests in mappings and bassMotion).
steps.reason: Definition of Done: build passes, no console errors.

filesChanged.change: Wire live listener to update signals in-flight
Scale pointer disturbance, jitter, and shimmer by motionAmplitude
New acceptance test file for US-024

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Reduced-motion preference is detected: Already implemented in signals.ts; enhanced with live listener (steps 1-2); tested in steps 6-7
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Motion amplitude is lowered: Already implemented for velocity; extended to pointer disturbance, jitter, and shimmer (steps 3-5); tested in step 6
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Core visual identity is preserved: motionAmplitude is 0.2, not 0 — particles still move, palette/density/complexity unchanged; verified in step 6 T-024-06
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.No separate page is required: Same mapSignalsToVisuals function handles both paths; verified in step 6 T-024-07

risks.risk: Over-dampening makes the experience feel static
risks.mitigation: motionAmplitude 0.2 still allows 20% motion; shimmer and jitter are dampened, not zeroed; colour and form remain fully active

risks.risk: Existing bassMotion.test.ts assertions may need adjustment after pointer/jitter changes
risks.mitigation: Step 8 runs the full suite; any failures from tighter motion will be caught and adjusted

dependenciesSatisfied.US-004: signals.ts with prefersReducedMotion detection is already merged
dependenciesSatisfied.US-009: particleField.ts geometry system with motionAmplitude consumption is already merged
